REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFFS BARBARA HAMMES, ALYSSA LANDY AND NOELLE NAREZ'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS' SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES TO RUSSELL FLYNN, SETS ONE AND TWO; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS OF $1,200.00 AGAINST DEFENDANT AND HIS COUNSEL OF RECORD CATHERINE GOLDEN IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO INTERROGATORY 1 (RESPONSE NEED ONLY INCLUDE DEFENDANT'S RESIDENTIAL PROPERTIES BUILT BEFORE 6/13/79) AND 37 (RESPONSE NEED ONLY LIST TENANTS WHO LIVED AT THE APARTMENT BUILDING FOR TWO YEARS BEFORE THE FIRE). MOTION IS DENIED AS TO INTERROGATORIES 38 AND 39 AND PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. THE REMAINDER OF THE MOTION IS MOOT IN LIGHT OF SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES. DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER IS SIGNED.(SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Plaintiffs Barbara Hammes, Alyssa Landy And Noelle Narez'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Special Interrogatories To Russell Flynn, Sets One And Two; Request For Sanctions Of $1,200.00 Against Defendant And His Counsel Of Record Catherine Golden
Off Calendar Sep-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mary Catherine Weiderhold, Esq.(415-533-0735) and Robert Simnth, Esq. (415-294-1465)for Plaintiff/Moving party Barbara Hammes, Alyssa Landy And Noelle Narez.
Catherine Golden, Esq. (415-981-6630) of Low Ball & Lynch for Defendant/opposing party Haight & Fillmore Associates LP.
Court reporter: Ken Brill, CSR #12797, (415-274-9977), Reported. (501).

9:33 am
The Court orders the parties to resolve the matter in the hallway.
10;13 am
The matter is recalled. Parties report the matter is resolved. The Court reads the tentative ruling. Plaintiff reports additional agreement. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with modification as follows:

Plaintiffs Barbara Hammes, Alyssa Landy And Noelle Narez'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Special Interrogatories To Russell Flynn, Sets One And Two; Request For Sanctions Of $1,200.00 Against Defendant And His Counsel Of Record Catherine Golden is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to Interrogatory 1 (response need only include Defendant's Residential properties built before 6/13/79) and 37 (response need only list tenants who lived at the apartment building for two years before the fire). Motion is denied as to Interrogatories 38 and 39 and Plaintiff's request for sanctions. The remainder of the motion is moot in light of supplemental responses. Defendant shall provide supplemental responses within 20 days of the date of this order. Order is signed.(see scanned order for details) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

Plaintiff Barbara Hammes' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Aug-07-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances on Friday, August 7, 2015:
Catherine E. Golden, Esq. of Low, Ball & Lynch (415.982.6300) for Defendants Haight & Fillmore Associates, LP; Russell b. Flynn; Russell B. Flynn 1990 Revocable Trust and Meridian Management Group Company;
No other appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Discovery.
(1)The Motion is denied without prejudice; (2) No sanctions are ordered; and (3) By on or before August 7, 2015, counsel for plaintiff Hammes and defendant Flynn are ordered to and shall conduct a comprehensive meet and confer at least by telephone and preferably in person to reach a mutually acceptable understanding on the production of the documents requested in the Request, taking into account,
a) there has probably been considerable duplication in the documents sought by the Request
and those sought from, and produced by, defendants Haight Fillmore and Meridian Management,
as well as those already produced by defendant Flynn;
b) the additional documents produced by defendants in May and June need to be taken into account
in the meet and confer discussions;
c) there are planned depositions by plaintiff that might assist in identifying and furnishing
with greater precision relevant documents to be produced;
d) after an agreement as to documents to be produced relevant to
the building in question, and, based on need and relevance, there might be considered an agreement
for the limited production of documents with respect to other buildings owned by defendant
Flynn, if in his possession and not produced by the other defendants; and
e) the "relevant period" for the document production for the building by Mr. Flynn
as to documents in his possession might more sensibly be limited
to no more than the four year period prior to the date of the fire in the building on September 27, 2011;
(4) By on or before August 7, 2015 the agreement with respect to document production under
the Request shall be reduced to writing; and

(5) By on or before August 15, 2015 the agreed on document production by defendant Flynn under the Request shall have occurred prior to plaintiff's planned depositions.

Order signed in open court. Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (501/REQ)
